channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Utah just passed the country’s first age-verification bill for app stores. But the fight isn’t over
item.description: On Wednesday, Utah became the first state in the country to pass legislation mandating that app stores verify users’ ages and get parental consent for certain activity on minors’ accounts.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This shaving brand was banned on TikTok, so it started an OnlyFans
item.description: How can a razor brand market its products online when the word razor is shadow banned? Start an OnlyFans, of course. London-based Nimbi, which launched at Erewhon in January 2024, began posting on TikTok last summer but noticed its views were disappointingly low.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Apple appeals UK encryption backdoor demand
item.description: Apple has publicly been pushing back against a UK order that demands it create encryption backdoors, and it has now formally appealed that order to the UK Investigatory Powers Tribunal.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JetZero teams with Delta to take the first blended-wing body commercial jetliner closer to reality
item.description: It’s tough enough bringing the first major redesign of a commercial jetliner to fruition—especially when it looks more like something out of The X-Files than a travel brochure.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Facebook’s AI slop hellscape is already here
item.description: Hello! Thank you, as always, for reading Plugged In. A quick scheduling note: Starting next week, this newsletter will land in your inbox on Fridays instead of Wednesdays. See you then.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: This $12,000 laptop comes with 24TB RAID-0 SSD storage, 128GB of RAM, and Intel's most powerful mobile CPU - but no Nvidia RTX 5090M GPU
item.description: Eurocom Raptor X17 pushes mobile workstation limits with 24TB storage, RTX 4090 power, and extreme performance capabilities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How CIOs can navigate a perfect digital storm of complexity, competition, and regulation
item.description: The banking, financial services, and insurance (BFSI) sector is facing a storm. In recent decades, the widespread adoption of the internet and the subsequent smartphone revolution have empowered consumers and businesses to look beyond incumbent providers of financial services.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Lawyers in DOGE challenge use Trump remarks before Congress in new filing
item.description: President Trump’s address to Congress on Tuesday night has already made its way into court, with a group of plaintiffs seeking to dismantle the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) quickly latching onto the remarks. "DOGE — perhaps you’ve heard of it. Perhaps," Trump said during his address.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: AMD RX 9070 could struggle to compete with Nvidia 50-series GPUs according to latest tech demo
item.description: New path tracing benchmarks have revealed that AMD's latest GPU generation struggles when weighed up against Nvidia's latest
item.dc:creator: alekshamcloughlin@outlook.com (Aleksha McLoughlin), TechRadar

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opera adds ‘Browser Operator,’ an AI agent, to its browser
item.description: Opera has announced that its browser is now equipped with Browser Operator, a built-in AI agent that can help users with a variety of tasks. For example, if an Opera user wants help buying a large pack of socks in a certain color or booking a flight, the user can ask Browser Operator can do it.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok’s ‘airport theory’ dares you to arrive just 15 minutes before your flight
item.description: When it comes to airports, travelers tend to fall into two camps. There are the anxious types who show up four hours early, with plenty of time to leisurely peruse duty-free and enjoy the airport lounge.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Advanced Technology Plays Key Role in Border Security
item.description: U.S.-Mexico border security relies on advanced technology, surveillance cameras, and drones to fight smuggling and drug trafficking, but data-sharing silos pose a significant challenge.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat questions Rubio over reported State Department Tesla purchase
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.) pressed the State Department on Monday for answers about the agency’s plans to purchase $400 million worth of armored Teslas after new reporting disputed its initial claims about the contract.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The Impact of Tariffs on Semiconductors: An Analysis
item.description: The impact of tariffs by the Trump Administration on semiconductors from Taiwan is a topic of debate among industry experts. In this article, we examine the potential effects of these tariffs and what it means for businesses and consumers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 5 questions about Trump’s crypto reserve plan
item.description: President Trump offered new details about his plan to create a strategic crypto reserve over the weekend, prompting the cryptocurrency market to rally after a few sluggish weeks.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tariffs an 'expensive game to play': Powerteam International CEO
item.description: Tariffs are going to be an "expensive game to play," Bill Walsh, CEO and founder of Powerteam International, said.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: TypeScript 5.8 reaches general availability
item.description: Microsoft’s TypeScript 5.8, the latest version of the company’s strongly typed superset of JavaScript, has reached general availability (GA).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Palo Alto expands secure private 5G network partnerships
item.description: Palo Alto Networks is looking to expand the role SASE plays in securing private 5G networks by collaborating with additional partners to offer end-to-end communications protection.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: UK data protection watchdog investigating how TikTok uses children's personal data
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The U.K.'s data protection watchdog said Monday that it's investigating how TikTok uses the personal information of teenagers to deliver content recommendations to them when they use the social media platform.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Breaking up Intel is the ‘dumbest idea’ around: Former CEO Craig Barett
item.description: In a blistering critique, former Intel CEO and Chairman Craig Barrett has strongly opposed breaking the company into separate design and foundry units, arguing that Intel’s recent technological resurgence positions it to challenge TSMC’s dominance in the semiconductor industry without corporate dism
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Extra cash with no catch? Spotting retiree job scams
item.description: Reports of task scams have significantly risen in the past four years, according to the Federal Trade Commission. Kurt the CyberGuy offers some red flags.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rust 1.85 arrives with long-awaited async closures
item.description: Rust 1.85, the latest version of the burgeoning language positioned for speed, safety, and ease of use, has arrived, with new capabilities including async closures. The release also constitutes a stabilization of the Rust 2024 edition of the language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What is an AI server? Why artificial intelligence needs specialized systems
item.description: AI servers are advanced computing systems designed to handle complex, resource-intensive AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World
